pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Grab local FTL hash correctly from FTL's own version output, and grab one digit less for remote hashes (also in debug log)

Signed-off-by: Adam Warner <me@adamwarner.co.uk>

Add support for RISC-V 64-bit installs

Signed-off-by: MichaIng <micha@dietpi.com>

basic-install.sh: Use `grep -E` instead of `egrep`

Signed-off-by: a1346054 <36859588+a1346054@users.noreply.github.com>

basic-install.sh: Use `command -v` instead of `which`

`command -v` is the standardized version of `which` and doesn't require
any extra packages

Signed-off-by: a1346054 <36859588+a1346054@users.noreply.github.com>

Update automated install/basic-install.sh

Co-authored-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>

Remove `@` and following character from interface name

Signed-off-by: RD WebDesign <github@rdwebdesign.com.br>

Replace `which` with `command -v` (#4499)

Signed-off-by: WaLLy3K WaLLy3K@users.noreply.github.com

use +short and omit obsolet awk

Co-authored-by: DL6ER <DL6ER@users.noreply.github.com>

Strip such lines starting with a #. Do not silently truncate lines if they have a # somewhere in between (like server=127.0.0.1#5353)

Signed-off-by: DL6ER <dl6er@dl6er.de>

Use the 'Location:' header only.

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -            if ! FTLlatesttag=$(curl -sI https://github.com/pi-hole/FTL/releases/latest | grep --color=never -i Location | awk -F / '{print $NF}' | tr -d '[:cntrl:]'); then
added_lines: +            if ! FTLlatesttag=$(curl -sI https://github.com/pi-hole/FTL/releases/latest | grep --color=never -i Location: | awk -F / '{print $NF}' | tr -d '[:cntrl:]'); then

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
read > mapfile as suggested by @dschaper

Signed-off-by: Adam Warner <me@adamwarner.co.uk>

Use ns1.pi-hole.net to resolve versions.pi-hole.net so that we do not see DNS cookie issues

Co-Authored-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
Signed-off-by: Adam Warner <me@adamwarner.co.uk>

Fix getting IP address in the presence of similar subnets

Change was suggested by @computergeek125 in #2387

Signed-off-by: Mcat12 <newtoncat12@yahoo.com>
removed_lines: -    IPV4_ADDRESS=$(ip -oneline -family inet address show | grep "${IPv4bare}" |  awk '{print $4}' | awk 'END {print}')
added_lines: +    IPV4_ADDRESS=$(ip -oneline -family inet address show | grep "${IPv4bare}/" |  awk '{print $4}' | awk 'END {print}')

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Only get the first gateway for chronometer

Signed-off-by: Mark Drobnak <mark.drobnak@gmail.com>
added_lines: +        net_gateway=$(ip route | grep default | cut -d ' ' -f 3 | head -n 1)

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
linting: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting

Signed-off-by: bcambl <blayne@blaynecampbell.com>
more shellcheck fixes

Let `ip` determine which links are candidates.

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -  availableInterfaces=$(ip -o link | grep -v "state DOWN\|lo" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)
added_lines: +  availableInterfaces=$(ip --oneline link show up | grep -v "lo" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Debian buildpack image for docker (Need `curl` for testing, using scm version to add `curl` and `git`.)

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
Improved regex: Remove all leading points from domain (.....xyz => .xyz). Return not only a part, but fill validated domain

Only ignore DOWN interfaces, `tun` interfaces are UNKNOWN.

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -  availableInterfaces=$(ip -o link | grep "state UP" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)
added_lines: +  availableInterfaces=$(ip -o link | grep -v "state DOWN\|lo" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Plain grep, escape the special characters.

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -    if iptables -S INPUT | head -n1 | grep -qv 'ACCEPT$' || iptables -S INPUT | tail -n1 | grep -qv '(^-A\|^-P).*ACCEPT$'; then
added_lines: +    if iptables -S INPUT | head -n1 | grep -qv '^-P.*ACCEPT$' || iptables -S INPUT | tail -n1 | grep -qv '^-\(A\|P\).*ACCEPT$'; then

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Check IPv6 to see if the route is reachable, if not, don't use.

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -  IPV6_ADDRESS=$(ip -6 route get 2001:4860:4860::8888 | awk -F " " '{ for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if ($i == "src") print $(i+1) }')
added_lines: +  IPV6_ADDRESS=$(ip -6 route get 2001:4860:4860::8888 | grep -v "unreachable" | awk -F " " '{ for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if ($i == "src") print $(i+1) }')

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Removed cat where we don't need it

Only allow UP interfaces to be selected. (DOWN interfaces would have not given IP addresses anyways.)

Signed-off-by: Dan Schaper <dan.schaper@pi-hole.net>
removed_lines: -  availableInterfaces=$(ip -o link | awk '{print $2}' | grep -v "lo" | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)
added_lines: +  availableInterfaces=$(ip -o link | grep "state UP" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d':' -f1 | cut -d'@' -f1)

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Look for "query[" instead of "query" to avoid false-positives, like listed below

root@raspberrypi:/etc/.pihole# grep 'query' /var/log/pihole.log | grep -v 'query\['
Dec 22 16:29:50 dnsmasq[30801]: forwarded code.jquery.com to 208.67.222.222
Dec 22 16:29:50 dnsmasq[30801]: reply code.jquery.com is <CNAME>
Dec 22 16:29:50 dnsmasq[30801]: reply code.jquery.netdna-cdn.com is 94.31.29.54
Dec 22 16:29:50 dnsmasq[30801]: reply code.jquery.netdna-cdn.com is 23.111.11.3
Dec 22 16:29:50 dnsmasq[30801]: reply code.jquery.netdna-cdn.com is 198.232.125.113
Remove grpping for today's log entries, because we flush the log every day
Don't forget to escape also the slash "/"
MERGE FAIL :ashamed_face:

Consistency

You know the drill
removed_lines: -	    piholeIPv6=$(ip -6 route get 2001:4860:4860::8888 | awk -F " " '{ for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if ($i == "src") print $(i+1) }')
added_lines: +		piholeIPv6=$(ip -6 route get 2001:4860:4860::8888 | awk -F " " '{ for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if ($i == "src") print $(i+1) }')

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Add OR TRUE to PKG_COUNT so that a 0 package to update doesn't grep -c to an exit value of 1.
Fix #718
added_lines: +	cat ${piholeDir}/${matterAndLight} | awk -F'#' '{print $1}' | awk '($1 !~ /^#/) { if (NF>1) {print $2} else {print $1}}' | sed -nr -e 's/\.{2,}/./g' -e '/\./p' >  ${piholeDir}/${supernova}

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: remove all mentions of spinner
Fix missing cats
added_lines: +				cat $blacklist | awk -v ipv4addr="$piholeIP" -v ipv6addr="$piholeIPv6" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print ipv4addr" "$0"\n"ipv6addr" "$0}' >> $adList

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Readded `cat` calls to fix permission issue
Readded `cat`s that were originally considered UUOC
added_lines: +    cat $piholeDir/$eventHorizon | awk -v ipv4addr="$piholeIP" -v ipv6addr="$piholeIPv6" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print ipv4addr" "$0"\n"ipv6addr" "$0}' >> $piholeDir/$accretionDisc

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Include fix for regex in verifyFreeDiskSpace()
Account for CHIP pc not having root on '/'

update verifyFreeDiskSpace()...

addresses #322. Will now check free space on the first file system output by 'df- lkP`, as opposed to `/` to make pi-hole more compatible with other distros.
Fix errant )
removed_lines: -	piholeIPCIDR=$(ip -o -f inet addr show dev $IPv4dev | awk '{print $4}') | awk 'END {print}')
added_lines: +	piholeIPCIDR=$(ip -o -f inet addr show dev $IPv4dev | awk '{print $4}' | awk 'END {print}')

pi-hole/pi-hole.message: Oneline ip link call, tighten awk call
Get last IP listed for interface
removed_lines: -	piholeIPCIDR=$(ip -o -f inet addr show dev $IPv4dev | awk '{print $4}')
added_lines: +	piholeIPCIDR=$(ip -o -f inet addr show dev $IPv4dev | awk '{print $4}') | sed -n '$p'

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
resolves #25

Will not count blank lines if they happen to exist.

Update chronometer.sh

Missed a change todaysAdsEliminated to /\/etc\/pihole\/gravity.list/

Removes more duplicates that uniq could not find

http://jacobsalmela.com/raspberry-pi-ad-blocker-advanced-setup/#comment-
1860675175

Thanks to napgravy for figuring this out.  It seems the DOS-style
line-endings that prevented uniq from getting rid of them.  This
reduces the ad domains from ~140,000 to around ~120,000 but it is much
more accurate.
added_lines: +cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt

pi-hole/pi-hole.message:
Fixes issue #2 whitelist support

Just put a file named whitelist.txt in your home folder.  This file
should contain one domain per line that needs to be whitelisted.  If
the file does not exists, the script will continue as normal.
removed_lines: -cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
added_lines: +	cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | grep -v -x -f $whitelist | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
+	cat /tmp/matter.txt | sed $'s/\r$//' | sort | uniq | sed '/^$/d' | awk -v "IP=$piholeIP" '{sub(/\r$/,""); print "address=/"$0"/"IP}' > /tmp/andLight.txt
